intro: Malachra ceratocarpa, also known as the prickly apple tree, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Malachra ceratocarpa include flavonoids, tannins, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
